affordances.name: Reversal-cost dependency audit
affordances.mechanism: When a platform, vendor, architecture, or migration decision is framed as reversible, require the answer to test whether integration debt, dual-run burden, retraining, contract sequencing, and local workarounds are quietly narrowing the future option set.

use_when: A vendor exit, platform shift, ERP migration, or infrastructure decision is being framed as normal implementation effort.
The team says it can switch later without testing whether the option survives time.
Dual-run coexistence, retraining, workarounds, or interface debt may be the hidden cost center.
do_not_use_when: The current path is deliberately sticky because stability, standardization, or commitment is still compounding advantage.
Switching later is being priced as if it were the same option as switching now.
The old architecture or belief is being defended mainly because departure feels costly, disloyal, or identity-threatening.
case_evidence_needed: The platform, vendor, architecture, or path being locked in.
The integration debt, contract dependencies, workflows, retraining, and workaround behavior created by the current path.
The cost of dual-run coexistence and rollback.
The evidence that the option to switch later remains alive or is already narrowing.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to price the reversal and coexistence costs before accepting claims that the team can switch later.
evidence_required: The dependencies and workflows that will harden over time.
The dual-run, rollback, support, retraining, and partner costs.
The trigger at which waiting destroys the option to switch.
good_output_shape: Separates useful stability from accidental dependency hardening.
States whether the later option is still alive.

diagnostic_questions: What dependency becomes harder to unwind each month we stay on this path?
Are we pricing dual-run coexistence and local workarounds, or only target build and cutover?
Is switching later still the same option as switching now?
misuse_guards: Do not treat future switching as equally cheap after custom workflows and contracts harden.
Do not defend an old architecture mainly because departure feels disloyal or identity-threatening.
Do not ignore productive lock-in when stability and standardization are deliberately creating advantage.

source_evidence.source_quote: persistent adherence to a previous state
reversal cost compounds faster than the migration plan acknowledges
source_evidence.section_hint: Additional Application Domain: Operational and Platform Lock-In

source_evidence.source_quote: Dual-run periods, exception handling, retraining, and partner workarounds can become the mechanism that locks the organization into the old path

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 5 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports an operational lock-in audit centered on reversal cost, dual-run traps, and dependency hardening.
dropped_material: PR58 promotes productive standardization as a separate bounded affordance because the source distinguishes deliberate sticky stability from accidental reversal-cost traps.
Did not extract AI persona consistency, mastery, or System 1 schema speed as standalone affordances.
Did not infer procurement or migration tactics beyond source support.
open_questions: Future packet comparison should test overlap between lock-in, path-dependence, commitment-bias, and status-quo-bias.

affordances.name: Productive standardization commitment
affordances.mechanism: When stability, standardization, or deep commitment is deliberately creating compounding advantage, require the answer to name what should stay sticky, why it lowers coordination cost or builds trust, and which boundaries must remain revisable.

use_when: Consistency, standardization, or deep commitment lowers coordination cost and improves execution quality over time.
Switching is expensive, novelty is low, and frequent reframing would damage trust, focus, or efficiency.
Leaders can consciously choose which norms, systems, or strategic positions should remain sticky and which must stay revisable.
do_not_use_when: The case is about reversal cost, dual-run burden, or hidden dependency hardening; use the reversal-cost dependency audit instead.
The environment has become novel enough that familiar schemas or frameworks may be misleading.
The current path is defended mainly because departure feels costly, disloyal, or identity-threatening.
case_evidence_needed: The norm, protocol, system, strategic position, or behavior proposed for deliberate stickiness.
The coordination cost, trust, execution quality, or compounding advantage created by consistency.
Evidence that novelty is low enough and switching is expensive enough to justify stability.
The explicit revisability boundary, review trigger, or escape condition.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to choose which commitments should remain stable, which should stay revisable, and what evidence would trigger reopening the lock-in.
evidence_required: The sticky commitment, norm, process, or strategic position.
The trust, coordination, or execution benefit of consistency.
The novelty, switching-cost, or stability evidence.
The review trigger or boundary that prevents stale lock-in.
good_output_shape: Explains the compounding advantage created by consistency.
Distinguishes deliberate stability from accidental dependency hardening.
States what remains revisable and when to reopen the choice.

diagnostic_questions: What exactly should remain sticky because consistency compounds value here?
What coordination cost, trust loss, or execution drag would frequent reframing create?
Which boundary or trigger keeps this deliberate lock-in from becoming stale rigidity?
misuse_guards: Do not use productive lock-in to defend stale architecture, strategy, or belief after fit changes.
Do not make everything sticky; name what stays revisable.
Do not confuse deliberate standardization with accidental dependency hardening.

source_evidence.source_quote: Most useful when stability creates compounding advantage
consistency, standardization, or deep commitment lowers coordination cost and improves execution quality over time
frequent reframing would destroy trust, focus, or efficiency
leaders consciously choose which norms, systems, or strategic positions should remain sticky and which must stay revisable

review_notes.normalization_note: PR58 targeted enrichment: the source supports productive lock-in as a separate receiver transaction from reversal-cost/dependency audit. This card is for deliberate stability, not accidental entrapment.
dropped_material: Kept AI persona consistency, mastery, and System 1 schema speed as examples or allies rather than separate runtime cards.
Kept multidisciplinary and first-principles material as guards for when sticky standards should be reopened.
open_questions: Packet stress should check whether productive lock-in remains subordinate to evidence of stability and does not overrule reversal-cost warnings.

absence_records.reason: The source says switching later should not be priced as if it were the same option after workflows, contracts, and operating habits harden.

source_evidence.source_quote: switching later is priced as if it were the same option as switching now

absence_records.reason: The source rejects defending an old architecture, strategy, or belief mainly because departure feels costly, disloyal, or identity-threatening.

source_evidence.source_quote: defend an old architecture, strategy, or belief mainly because departure feels costly, disloyal, or identity-threatening
silently losing freedom while the team still talks as if freedom is intact
source_evidence.section_hint: Additional Application Domain: Operational and Platform Lock-In

source_evidence.source_quote: Most useful when a migration plan treats reversal as available even though dependency drag is already compounding

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 5 source reading found strong reviewed depth for Lock-In. PR58 adds productive standardization as a second, source-backed transaction while preserving the reversal-cost/dependency audit.
dropped_material: AI persona consistency, mastery, and System 1 schema speed remain context rather than separate positive runtime affordances.
No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: Later packet comparison should verify whether this card and path-dependence can coexist without duplicate bulk.
